REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, SUPERIOR COURT RECEIVER KEVIN SINGER'S AMENDED NOTICE OF HEARING RE: SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. MOVING PARTY CARRIED ITS BURDEN UNDER THE FIRST PRONG OF THE ANTI-SLAPP. DEFENDANT'S MOTION WAS TIMELY FILED ON JUNE 9, 2023 (RE-NOTICED ON JUNE 30, 2023) IN ACCORDANCE WITH APRIL 7, 2023 SERVICE DATE (SINGH DECLARATION, PARAGRAPH 15). THE COMPLAINT SUBJECT TO THIS MOTION WAS SERVED, BUT NOT PROPERLY FILED. ON APRIL 8, 2023 PLAINTIFF SUBMITTED A STIPULATION WITHOUT A PROPOSED ORDER (NOT FOLLOWING THE "STIPULATION AND ORDER" PROCEDURE REGARDING THE FILING TO THE COMPLAINT). ON APRIL 24, 2023 THE CLERK REJECTED THE FILING WITH INSTRUCTIONS TO OBTAIN A COURT ORDER. PLAINTIFF APPEARS TO HAVE FOLLOWED THOSE INSTRUCTIONS MONTHS LATER, ON JULY 24, 2023 BY PRESENTING AN EX-PARTE APPLICATION TO FILE A CORRECTED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT PURSUANT TO A STIPULATION. HAD PLAINTIFF TIMELY AVAILED ITSELF OF THE AVAILABLE PROCEDURES FOR OBTAINING AN ORDER (STIPULATION AND ORDER, EX-PARTE OR MOTION) THE PROCEDURAL ISSUE REGARDING SERVING, BUT NOT FILING A CORRECTED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, COULD HAVE BEEN AVOIDED OR RESOLVED WELL IN ADVANCE OF THE HEARING AND THE BRIEFING DEADLINES. THE ISSUE OF THE CORRECTED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS RESOLVED BY JULY 24, 2023 EX-PARTE AND THE COURT ALLOWS THE FILING OF THE CORRECTED FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT PER STIPULATION OF THE PARTIES NUNC PRO TUNC TO THE ORIGINAL DATE OF THE FILING OF THE STIPULATION, I.E. APRIL 8, 2023. LATE-FILED OPPOSITION IS STRICKEN. ALL THE ARGUMENTS MADE IN THE LATE-FILED OPPOSITION COULD HAVE BEEN PRESENTED 9 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING EVEN WITH THE PROCEDURAL POSTURE OF THE CASE CREATED AND NOT RECTIFIED BY THE PLAINTIFF STARTING WITH THE FILING OF A WRONG COMPLAINT ON APRIL 3, 2023 UNTIL JULY 24, 2023. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPO

LAW AND MOTION 302, JUL-10-2023. HEARING RE: COURT-APPOINTED RECEIVER KEVIN SINGERS' MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. RULING - DISCUSSED. COUNSEL ADVISES THE COURT THAT AN AMENDED MOTION HAS BEEN FILED AND RE-NOTICED FOR JUL-28-2023. AT THE CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSION, THE COURT ORDERS THE FOLLOWING: "SUPERIOR COURT RECEIVER KEVIN SINGER'S" UNOPPOSED "SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT" SHALL BE HEARD ON JUL-28-2023 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 501 - SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR THIS DATE - JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Aug-08-2024 Continued To Oct-23-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Final Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-07-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES:
Atty. KAREN Y. UCHIYAMA, Esq. of Law Offices of Karen Y. Uchiyama (Tel: 415-271-3364) for plaintiff;
Atty. DANA M. ANDREOLI, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-421-3400) for defendants 1901 Pacific LLC and Wisfe Aish (via Zoom videoconference).

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT 1901 PACIFIC LLC, and WISFE AISH's Motion For Entry Of Final Judgment.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed parties to appear.

Matter argued and submitted.

The Court rules as follows: Defendants' motion for entry of final judgment is GRANTED.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order for review/signature.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion An Motion For Entry Of Final Judgment Declaration Of Blake Alsbrook In Support There Of
Off Calendar Jul-08-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. BLAKE C. ALSBROOK, Esq. of Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP (Tel: 310-273-6333) for intervening defendant Kevin Singer;
Atty. DANA M. ANDREOLI, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (Tel: 415-421-3400) for defendants 1901 Pacific LLC and Wisfe Aish.

HEARING RE: INTERVENING DEFENDANT KEVIN SINGER's Motion For Entry Of Final Judgment.

Matter argued and submitted.

The Court indicates that if defendants 1901 Pacific LLC and Wisfe Aish wish to have final judgment entered as to them, they must bring their own motion.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Intervening defendant Kevin Singer's motion for final judgment is granted.

Under CCP 581(f)(3), the court may dismiss the complaint as to a defendant "[a]fter a motion to strike the whole of a complaint is granted without leave to amend and either party moves for dismissal." The anti-SLAPP statute provides no mechanism for granting leave to amend. (Martin v. Inland Empire Utilities Agency (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 611, 629.) An order of dismissal operates as a final judgment with respect to the dismissed defendant. (Desai v. Farmers Ins. Exchange (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 1110.)

On August 8, 2023, the court granted Singer's anti-SLAPP motion to strike the entire first amended complaint ("FAC") without leave to amend. Accordingly, the court enters final judgment of dismissal of the FAC with prejudice as to defendant Singer.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: S. Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Atty. DANA ANDREOLI, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (tel: 415-421-3400) for Defendants 1901 Pacific LLC and Wisfe Aish.
Atty. BLAKE ALSBROOK, Esq. of Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP (tel: 310-273-6333) for Defendant/Receiver Kevin Singer.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT 1901 PACIFIC LLC And WISFE AISH's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

Matter discussed. The Court recommends defendants to file a motion for final judgment. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion for judgment on the pleadings of the first amended complaint (FAC) is ordered off calendar as moot. The FAC was stricken by the court's August 8, 2023 anti-SLAPP order, so no pleadings exist to be adjudged.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Sonia Singh; Ervin Cohen & Jessup LLP; 310-273-6333; appearing as counsel for defendant and cross-complainant San Francisco Superior Court Receiver Kevin Singer.
Karen Y. Uchiyama; Law Offices of Karen Y. Uchiyama; 415-563-9300; appearing as counsel for plaintiff Verpresar, LLC.

Plaintiff contests the court's tentative ruling. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

SUPERIOR COURT RECEIVER KEVIN SINGER'S AMENDED NOTICE OF HEARING RE: SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is GRANTED. Moving party carried its burden under the first prong of the Anti-Slapp. Defendant's motion was timely filed on June 9, 2023 (re-noticed on June 30, 2023) in accordance with April 7, 2023 service date (Singh Declaration, paragraph 15). The complaint subject to this motion was served, but not properly filed. On April 8, 2023 Plaintiff submitted a stipulation without a proposed order (not following the "Stipulation and order" procedure regarding the filing to the complaint). On April 24, 2023 the clerk rejected the filing with instructions to obtain a court order. Plaintiff appears to have followed those instructions months later, on July 24, 2023 by presenting an ex-parte application to file a Corrected First Amended Complaint pursuant to a stipulation. Had Plaintiff timely availed itself of the available procedures for obtaining an order (stipulation and order, ex-parte or motion) the procedural issue regarding serving, but not filing a Corrected First Amended Complaint, could have been avoided or resolved well in advance of the hearing and the briefing deadlines. The issue of the Corrected First Amended Complaint was resolved by July 24, 2023 ex-parte and the Court allows the filing of the Corrected First Amended Complaint per stipulation of the parties nunc pro tunc to the original date of the filing of the stipulation, i.e. April 8, 2023.
Late-filed opposition is stricken. All the arguments made in the late-filed opposition could have been presented 9 court days before the hearing even with the procedural posture of the case created and not rectified by the Plaintiff starting with the filing of a wrong complaint on April 3, 2023 until July 24, 2023.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
